content: Just as language education leverages explicit vocabulary learning of 'reflexion' to train human learners to intentionally pause and reflect on their own reasoning, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by adding a structured 'terminology induction' pre-training step that teaches LLMs to explicitly label different types of self-reflective cognitive states, rather than only asking them to implicitly critique outputs. This framing turns the generic ability to self-reflect into a learnable, labeled skill for LLMs, mirroring how humans learn to formalize reflection through language education.
